original_hypothesis.title: Admin Can Extract 100% of Trade Value via Maximum Fees
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The admin can set both buyerFee and sellerFee to 50% (5000 bps). This results in the buyer paying 1.5x the base amount and the seller receiving 0.5x the base amount, effectively allowing the platform to extract 100% of the base trade value.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While capped at 50% individually, the combined effect of a 50% buyer fee and 50% seller fee means the protocol takes an amount equal to the entire base value of the trade. This extreme fee extraction can be implemented instantly without a timelock.
deployment_assumptions: Admin sets fees to maximum allowed limits
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: setBuyerFee and setSellerFee allow up to 5000 bps

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(_amount <= 5000); allows 50% fee on both sides

preconditions: Admin maliciously or accidentally sets both fees to 5000
Users execute trades before noticing the fee change

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Front-running user trades with fee updates (if mempool exists)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Protocol extracts massive value from unaware users

economic_questions: Are fee changes broadcasted with a delay?
Do frontends warn users of high slippage/fees?
validation_requirements: Calculate exact payout ratios at max fees

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: 100% of trade base value per transaction

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Destroys user trust and marketplace liquidity

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Volume of trades executed under max fees

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
